seoTitle: How to View Solved Tickets in Zendesk Views
metaDescription: Learn how to view solved tickets in Zendesk using the built-in Recently Solved view or by building a custom filtered view with your own conditions.
intro: Once a ticket is marked solved, it shouldn't disappear from view entirely — agents often need to look back at resolved cases for follow-ups or context. This guide shows how to use Zendesk's pre-built solved ticket view and how to build a custom one with your own filters.

steps.heading: Open the Views panel
steps.body: Move to the left sidebar panel and click the list-shaped Views icon.

steps.heading: Use the standard solved view
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of the list and select the standard Recently Solved Tickets view.

steps.heading: Or create a custom view
steps.body: Click Manage Views to navigate to Admin Center and set up a custom queue instead.

steps.heading: Name and configure the view
steps.body: Click Create View, input an explicit title, and configure the conditions panel to require Status Is Solved and Assignee Is Current User.

steps.heading: Add relevant columns
steps.body: Add table columns for Subject, Requester, and Date fields, then click Save.

steps.heading: Filter by group or date range
steps.body: Return to your workspace sidebar to launch and further filter the completed case logs by group assignment or custom date ranges.

faq.question: Do solved tickets disappear from Zendesk after resolution?
faq.answer: No, they move into a static but still searchable state and remain accessible through views.

faq.question: Can I filter solved tickets by a specific agent?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set a condition requiring the assignee to be a specific agent when building a custom view.

faq.question: What columns are useful to add to a solved tickets view?
faq.answer: Subject, Requester, and Solve Date are commonly used columns for quickly scanning resolved cases.

faq.question: Can I reopen a solved ticket from this view?
faq.answer: Yes, opening a solved ticket lets you change its status again if follow-up is needed.
